questions.question: What is a market?
questions.correct_answer: A place where buyers and sellers come together to buy and sell goods and services
questions.explanation: Markets connect people who want to sell products with those who want to buy them.

questions.question: What is a weekly market?
questions.correct_answer: A market that is held once a week in a particular area
questions.explanation: Traders set up temporary shops and sell goods such as vegetables, clothes, and household items.

questions.question: Why are goods usually cheaper in weekly markets?
questions.correct_answer: Because traders do not have to pay permanent shop rent
questions.explanation: Lower costs allow sellers to offer goods at lower prices.

questions.question: What is a neighbourhood market?
questions.correct_answer: A market with shops located near residential areas selling daily necessities
questions.explanation: These markets provide easy access to items such as groceries, milk, and vegetables.

questions.question: What are shopping complexes or malls?
questions.correct_answer: Large buildings with many shops selling branded and modern products
questions.explanation: They offer a variety of goods such as clothes, electronics, and food items.

questions.question: Who are retailers?
questions.correct_answer: Sellers who sell goods directly to consumers in small quantities
questions.explanation: Retailers usually buy goods from wholesalers and sell them in local shops.

questions.question: Who are wholesalers?
questions.correct_answer: Traders who buy goods in large quantities from producers and sell them to retailers
questions.explanation: They help distribute goods to different markets.

questions.question: What is meant by the chain of markets?
questions.correct_answer: The link of markets through which goods move from producers to consumers
questions.explanation: Goods pass through different sellers before reaching the final buyer.

questions.question: Why do the prices of goods vary in different markets?
questions.correct_answer: Because of differences in transport costs, rent, and profit margins
questions.explanation: Additional expenses can increase the selling price of goods.

questions.question: Why are markets important in daily life?
questions.correct_answer: They make goods and services available to people
questions.explanation: Markets help connect producers and consumers and support economic activity.

notes.title: Weekly Markets
notes.content: Held once a week and offer a wide variety of goods at relatively lower prices.

notes.title: Neighbourhood Markets
notes.content: Provide everyday items close to residential areas for convenience.

notes.title: Retailers
notes.content: Sell goods directly to customers in small quantities.

notes.title: Wholesalers
notes.content: Purchase goods in bulk from producers and supply them to retailers.

notes.title: Chain of Markets
notes.content: Shows how goods move from producers to wholesalers, retailers, and finally consumers.

facts.fact: Weekly markets usually have many traders selling different types of goods in one place.
Neighbourhood shops are convenient because they are located near people's homes.
Goods often pass through several traders before reaching consumers.
Markets play an important role in distributing goods from producers to consumers.
